name: Byars, Arthur Forest
motto: If words be made of breath, and breath of life, I have no life to breathe.
description: Will dig artesian wells to run electric fly fans.
text:
If words be made of breath, and breath of life, I have no life to breathe.
Will dig artesian wells to run electric fly fans.

name: Cleveland, Arthur Franklin
motto: He, wand'ring long, a wider circle made, And many languaged nations has survey'd.
description: Cleveland will run a co-operative creamery at Spartanburg, S.C.
text:
He, wand'ring long, a wider circle made, And many languaged nations has survey'd.
Cleveland will run a co-operative creamery at Spartanburg, S.C.

name: Ellison, Alfred Gaillard
motto: The reason firm, the temperate will, Endurance, foresight, strength, and skill.
description: Always having an athletic turn, he will coach the Mt. Zion football team and own a baseball franchise at Winnsboro, S.C.
text:
The reason firm, the temperate will, Endurance, foresight, strength, and skill.
Always having an athletic turn, he will coach the Mt. Zion football team and own a baseball franchise at Winnsboro, S.C.

name: Hill, David Hugh
motto: Dreaming, dreaming life away!
description: Will write a song entitled, "Ain't She Fine?" and then go to sleep whistling it.
text:
Dreaming, dreaming life away!
Will write a song entitled, "Ain't She Fine?" and then go to sleep whistling it.

name: Jacobs, Thomas Boyd
motto: My words fly up, my thoughts remain below.
description: He will live with the hope of improving the Newberry reputation.
text:
My words fly up, my thoughts remain below.
He will live with the hope of improving the Newberry reputation.

name: Johnson, John Evander
motto: Too little known to be appreciated, Too retired to win renown!
description: Having many traits of a Good Samaritan, and being very ladylike, he will win fame as a Red Cross nurse.
text:
Too little known to be appreciated, Too retired to win renown!
Having many traits of a Good Samaritan, and being very ladylike, he will win fame as a Red Cross nurse.

name: Keitt, Thomas Ellison
motto: "To know, to esteem, to love—and then to part."
description: Keitt will some day be chief chemist at Clemson College.
text:
"To know, to esteem, to love—and then to part."
Keitt will some day be chief chemist at Clemson College.

name: Latimer, William James
motto: "The feast of reason, and the flow of soul."
description: A farmer in Abbeville County.
text:
"The feast of reason, and the flow of soul."
A farmer in Abbeville County.

name: Luther, Bostick Martin
motto: His path lies over the mansion lawn.
description: He will organize a loafers' club and become the president of it.
text:
His path lies over the mansion lawn.
He will organize a loafers' club and become the president of it.

name: John, Hugh McClain
motto: But thou bringst valor, too, with wit; Two things that seldom fail to hit.
clubs: Recording Secretary and President, the Columbian Society
description: "Joe" will be advance agent for the "Clemson Literary Club," and will marry early in life.
text:
But thou bringst valor, too, with wit; Two things that seldom fail to hit.
"Joe" will be advance agent for the "Clemson Literary Club," and will marry early in life.

name: Moore, William Cash
motto: A cheerful life devoid of care?
description: Will study the economic value of the Maypop.
text:
A cheerful life devoid of care?
Will study the economic value of the Maypop.

name: Moss, James Michael Jr.
motto: And his sunny locks hang on his temples like a golden fleece.
description: Moss will be chief "sheep shearer" on a Western ranch.
text:
And his sunny locks hang on his temples like a golden fleece.
Moss will be chief "sheep shearer" on a Western ranch.

name: Charles, Kuh Politzer.
motto: "Doubling his pleasures, and his cares dividing."
description: Polly will be a diamond dealer, and sell peanuts and popcorn at the baseball games.
text:
"Doubling his pleasures, and his cares dividing."
Polly will be a diamond dealer, and sell peanuts and popcorn at the baseball games.

name: Lawrence, Geddings Southard.
motto: "The man who has a tongue, I say, is no man. If with his tongue he cannot win a woman."
description: He will manage everything he comes in contact with, except Dr. and Mrs. Southard.
text:
"The man who has a tongue, I say, is no man. If with his tongue he cannot win a woman."
He will manage everything he comes in contact with, except Dr. and Mrs. Southard.

name: Stokes, Thomas Ervin
motto: "The pen is mightier than the sword, but I am both."
description: Being a great orator, he will follow the plow.
text:
"The pen is mightier than the sword, but I am both."
Being a great orator, he will follow the plow.

name: Stuckey, Henry Perkins
motto: "Let me be no assistant for a State, But keep a farm and pasture great."
description: Stuckey will make hay while the sun shines.
text:
"Let me be no assistant for a State, But keep a farm and pasture great."
Stuckey will make hay while the sun shines.

name: Summers, John Clifford
motto: For courage mounteth with occasion.
clubs: Secretary and Vice-President, Y. M. C. A.
description: Will raise sweet-scented Bermuda grass and registered Jersey cows in Orangeburg County.
text:
For courage mounteth with occasion.
Will raise sweet-scented Bermuda grass and registered Jersey cows in Orangeburg County.

name: Winters, Rhett Yeoman
motto: Man is immortal till his work is done.
description: Woe to the grasshopper that crosses his path, for he will be a bug catcher.
text:
Man is immortal till his work is done.
Woe to the grasshopper that crosses his path, for he will be a bug catcher.

name: Adams, David Gregg
motto: Thy modesty is a candle to thy merit.
description: "Shoat" will survey the situation at Converse College.
text:
Thy modesty is a candle to thy merit.
"Shoat" will survey the situation at Converse College.

name: Alford, Emanuel Peachman
motto: They also serve, who only stand and wait.
description: Will be Superintendent of Education of Florence County.
text:
They also serve, who only stand and wait.
Will be Superintendent of Education of Florence County.

name: Brunson, Artemas Lowe, Jr.
motto: I view, by no presumption led, your revels of the night.
description: Received his noble start in the educational line at the South Carolina Co-Ed Institute. Since then has made quite a hit as a second to George Washington. Will survey a path through the Blue Ridge Mountains.
text:
I view, by no presumption led, your revels of the night.
Received his noble start in the educational line at the South Carolina Co-Ed Institute. Since then has made quite a hit as a second to George Washington. Will survey a path through the Blue Ridge Mountains.

name: Bull, Izard Witte
motto: Thy long legs betoken thy power of gaining ground.
description: Received his early education in the Town of Vances; feeling that his was to be a higher calling, entered Clemson. He will be happy, for all his troubles will be little ones.
text:
Thy long legs betoken thy power of gaining ground.
Received his early education in the Town of Vances; feeling that his was to be a higher calling, entered Clemson. He will be happy, for all his troubles will be little ones.

name: Dominic, Ferdinand Cherry
motto: He will succeed on merit all his own.
description: He will go to Panama and become a rich man.
text:
He will succeed on merit all his own.
He will go to Panama and become a rich man.

name: Augustus, Law Ervin
motto: For love will still be lord of all.
description: Gus will develop water-power on Black Creek and then marry someone "across the way."
text:
For love will still be lord of all.
Gus will develop water-power on Black Creek and then marry someone "across the way."

name: Goodwin, Thomas Lake
motto: Declining into the vale of years.
description: Goodwin will be a local surveyor.
text:
Declining into the vale of years.
Goodwin will be a local surveyor.

name: Hoyt, Lew Rice
motto: A merry heart maketh a glad countenance.
description: Will be President of Southern Branch of Railroad Civil Engineers. By Editor-in-Chief.
text:
A merry heart maketh a glad countenance.
Will be President of Southern Branch of Railroad Civil Engineers. By Editor-in-Chief.

name: Jenkins, Henry Samuel
motto: I have no common sense; mine is all extraordinary.
description: The civil engineer of the future.
text:
I have no common sense; mine is all extraordinary.
The civil engineer of the future.

name: Phillips, James Vernon
motto: Thy voice is a celestial melody.
description: Behold! Another Demosthenes!
text:
Thy voice is a celestial melody.
Behold! Another Demosthenes!

name: Reid, John Hamilton
motto: Little things are great to little men.
description: He will migrate to Russia, where he will become a socialistic leader.
text:
Little things are great to little men.
He will migrate to Russia, where he will become a socialistic leader.

name: Sweeny, Francis Raymond Izlar
motto: Music has charms to soothe the savage, Split a brick, and burst a cabbage.
description: Sweeny will be a great orchestra leader.
text:
Music has charms to soothe the savage, Split a brick, and burst a cabbage.
Sweeny will be a great orchestra leader.

name: Sanders, Wade Arlington
motto: By his excessive laughter you will know him.
description: Sanders will be found waiting at the station when the trains come along.
text:
By his excessive laughter you will know him.
Sanders will be found waiting at the station when the trains come along.

name: Webb, States Lockwood
motto: If silence is golden, then you are bankrupt!
description: "Runt" Will some day build a footpath.
text:
If silence is golden, then you are bankrupt!
"Runt" Will some day build a footpath.

name: Woodroof, Jack Lowe
motto: I'll put a girdle 'round about the earth in forty minutes.
description: His present address is Savannah, Ga. Will be a railroad surveyor.
text:
I'll put a girdle 'round about the earth in forty minutes.
His present address is Savannah, Ga. Will be a railroad surveyor.

name: Adams, Patrick Henry
motto: First or not at all.
description: Present address is Greenwood, S.C. Pat will achieve fame with the General Electric Company.
text:
First or not at all.
Present address is Greenwood, S.C. Pat will achieve fame with the General Electric Company.

name: Barton, Theophilus Fisk, Jr.
motto: "The glass of fashion, and the mold of form."
description: His early education was received at the Orangeburg Graded School; and, with this as a foundation, he entered Freshman class September 9th, 1902. Played on class football team, 1901-1905, 1905-1906, and is Secretary of the Annual staff, 1906; also one of the literary editors. Will teach Military Science.
text:
"The glass of fashion, and the mold of form."
His early education was received at the Orangeburg Graded School; and, with this as a foundation, he entered Freshman class September 9th, 1902. Played on class football team, 1901-1905, 1905-1906, and is Secretary of the Annual staff, 1906; also one of the literary editors. Will teach Military Science.

name: Baskin, William Stuart
motto: "When Night has set her silvery lamp on high, Then is the time to study."
description: Baskin was born at Lowndesville, S.C., on the 1st of February, 1884; but, since then, has moved to Iva, S.C. Early education received at the Lowndesville High School, and entered the Freshman Class at Clemson January 28th, 1903. Played on class football team, 1904-1905, 1905-1906. Held the office of Treasurer. Chief electrician at Monterey, S.C.
text:
"When Night has set her silvery lamp on high, Then is the time to study."
Baskin was born at Lowndesville, S.C., on the 1st of February, 1884; but, since then, has moved to Iva, S.C. Early education received at the Lowndesville High School, and entered the Freshman Class at Clemson January 28th, 1903. Played on class football team, 1904-1905, 1905-1906. Held the office of Treasurer. Chief electrician at Monterey, S.C.

name: Beckett, William
motto: To scorn delight, and live laborious days.
description: Practical agriculture will be the height of his fame.
text:
To scorn delight, and live laborious days.
Practical agriculture will be the height of his fame.

name: Boesch, John Christian
motto: Take life too seriously, and what is it worth?
description: Boesch will be the first of the class to get married.
text:
Take life too seriously, and what is it worth?
Boesch will be the first of the class to get married.

name: Cadwallader, Coles.
motto: It is better to be born lucky than rich.
description: Will be the popular Manager of the Atlanta baseball team.
text:
It is better to be born lucky than rich.
Will be the popular Manager of the Atlanta baseball team.

name: Thomas, Robertson Ellison.
motto: True as the dial to the sun.
description: Will own the Winnsboro electrical plant.
text:
True as the dial to the sun.
Will own the Winnsboro electrical plant.

name: Gelzer, Jennings Axson
motto: I am a pretty, handsome boy, just fit for a lady's toy.
description: Another pole climber.
text:
I am a pretty, handsome boy, just fit for a lady's toy.
Another pole climber.

name: Harper, Samuel Paul
motto: He that complies against his will is of his own opinion still.
clubs: Secretary and Critic, the Calhoun Society
President, College Press Association of South Carolina
description: Harper will be editor of a country newspaper.
text:
He that complies against his will is of his own opinion still.
Harper will be editor of a country newspaper.

name: Holland, George Broyles
motto: A life in which nothing happened.
description: Will break some poor girl's heart.
text:
A life in which nothing happened.
Will break some poor girl's heart.

name: Jones, Earle Hemphill
motto: The busy hum of men.
description: Jones will be city electrician of Abbeville, S.C.
text:
The busy hum of men.
Jones will be city electrician of Abbeville, S.C.

name: Rinehart, Kennie Oscar
motto: Thy pathway is among the stars.
description: He will reach the pinnacle of his fame at the top of an electric light pole.
text:
Thy pathway is among the stars.
He will reach the pinnacle of his fame at the top of an electric light pole.

name: Savage, Marion Alexander
motto: I have set my life upon a cast, And I will stand the hazard of the die.
description: Savage will get his commission in the army.
text:
I have set my life upon a cast, And I will stand the hazard of the die.
Savage will get his commission in the army.

name: Alexander, Raymond McAlily
motto: A good, strong character, with independence and force.
description: "Me" will be an old-fashioned school teacher.
text:
A good, strong character, with independence and force.
"Me" will be an old-fashioned school teacher.

name: William, Alger Putnam
motto: No more of that, Will, an thou lovest me.
clubs: Left tackle, class football team, '05 and '06
description: He will live on water and wild grass.
text:
No more of that, Will, an thou lovest me.
He will live on water and wild grass.

name: Schumpet, Homer Wilbur
motto: I have often regretted my silence; never, my speech.
description: Not being satisfied with country life, moved to the town of Newberry. Early education received at the Dead Fall School, but escaped with his life and entered the Freshman Class. The first shall be last, and the last shall be first.
text:
I have often regretted my silence; never, my speech.
Not being satisfied with country life, moved to the town of Newberry. Early education received at the Dead Fall School, but escaped with his life and entered the Freshman Class. The first shall be last, and the last shall be first.

name: Smith, William Ralph
motto: Most musical, and most melancholy.
description: Early education received at the Charleston High School, from which he entered the Freshman Class at Clemson. Present address, Charleston, S.C. His name is on the honor roll, therefore he will do nothing.
text:
Most musical, and most melancholy.
Early education received at the Charleston High School, from which he entered the Freshman Class at Clemson. Present address, Charleston, S.C. His name is on the honor roll, therefore he will do nothing.

name: White, William Porter
motto: And he is the wisest of our race.
description: A consulting engineer, with headquarters in Atlanta, Ga.
text:
And he is the wisest of our race.
A consulting engineer, with headquarters in Atlanta, Ga.

name: Wright, John Nichols
motto: I do perceive here a divided duty.
description: Whatever he does, he will always be Wright.
text:
I do perceive here a divided duty.
Whatever he does, he will always be Wright.

name: Derrick, Oscar Luther
motto: Whatever he did was done with so much grace.
description: He has made quite a record as a football player. In his first year, he played scrub ball; but since then, has been one of the stars on the varsity, All-Southern tackle for two years, by which team he was elected Captain in '05-'06. He will be a great football coach.
text:
Whatever he did was done with so much grace.
He has made quite a record as a football player. In his first year, he played scrub ball; but since then, has been one of the stars on the varsity, All-Southern tackle for two years, by which team he was elected Captain in '05-'06. He will be a great football coach.

name: Dickson, Laurie Craig
motto: Not to know me, argues yourself unknown.
description: He played on class football team of '03, scrubs of '04, and varsity of '05. Dickson will break his neck at the first false step.
text:
Not to know me, argues yourself unknown.
He played on class football team of '03, scrubs of '04, and varsity of '05. Dickson will break his neck at the first false step.

name: Grainger, Charles Ayres
motto: "The scattered remnants of a poet, all in one finished whole."
clubs: Columbian Society: Reporting and Prosecuting Critic, Secretary, Vice-President, and President
description: Although a "Yap," he will reach the height of his fame between the plow-handles.
text:
"The scattered remnants of a poet, all in one finished whole."
Although a "Yap," he will reach the height of his fame between the plow-handles.

name: Johnson, Samuel Lewis
motto: "Youth on the prow, and pleasure at the helm."
clubs: Art editor of the Annual of 1906
description: Will always deal in cotton mill catalogues.
text:
"Youth on the prow, and pleasure at the helm."
Will always deal in cotton mill catalogues.

name: John, Klinck.
motto: He sings a little bass, a little baritone.
clubs: Chief Rooter, baseball and football teams
description: Great will be his footprints on the sands of time.
text:
He sings a little bass, a little baritone.
Great will be his footprints on the sands of time.

name: Fred, B. McLaurin.
motto: To be good, rather than to be conspicuous.
description: "Mc" will astound the literary world with his poetry.
text:
To be good, rather than to be conspicuous.
"Mc" will astound the literary world with his poetry.

name: Raucil, John Junior
motto: My man's as true as steel.
description: His early education was received at the Corinth School; there he received such advanced learning that he entered "Prep" September 11th, 1902. He played on the class football team for three years. Later he deserted the class team and played on varsity, 1905-1906. Will be floor sweeper in the Saluda cotton mills.
text:
My man's as true as steel.
His early education was received at the Corinth School; there he received such advanced learning that he entered "Prep" September 11th, 1902. He played on the class football team for three years. Later he deserted the class team and played on varsity, 1905-1906. Will be floor sweeper in the Saluda cotton mills.

name: Whiteside, Albert Newton
motto: Come together, my merry men.
description: He received his early education at the Lewisville High School, but, being desirous of a higher education, he entered Clemson College September 11th, 1902. Another Yap.
text:
Come together, my merry men.
He received his early education at the Lewisville High School, but, being desirous of a higher education, he entered Clemson College September 11th, 1902. Another Yap.
